skill.name: architectural-forensics
skill.description: Analyze AI agent frameworks with a structured protocol that separates implementation architecture from cognitive design patterns.
skill.summary: Deconstruct agent framework architecture and synthesize reusable design guidance.
skill.author: Dowwie

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many potential issues, but review found the high-risk weak-cryptography detections are false positives in prose, diagrams, markdown filenames, and illustrative schemas. The skill is documentation-only and has no executable package code, network calls, secret access, or prompt injection attempts. It still merits low-risk disclosure because it instructs agents to run local mapping commands and read and write workspace analysis artifacts.

low_findings.title: Documented Local Command Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill includes markdown examples for running a codebase mapping script, creating output directories, and resetting interrupted jobs. These are not hidden payloads or executable package code, but users should review paths before running local commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are visible markdown workflow instructions with ordinary local commands. I found no evidence of command obfuscation, remote payload download, or user-input shell interpolation.

low_findings.title: Workspace File Read and Write Instructions
low_findings.description: The skill directs agents to read repository maps, source clusters, extracts, and reports, then write analysis artifacts under forensics-output and reports. This filesystem use is central to the skill but should be disclosed because it can process large local codebases.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple role files explicitly define bounded read and write locations. The locations are workspace analysis artifacts, not sensitive system paths or credential stores.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography alerts occur on prose, markdown structure, or illustrative schema text rather than cryptographic APIs. I found no MD5, SHA-1, cipher, certificate parsing, or password handling logic in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are markdown instructions and examples, not executable cryptographic code. Targeted review did not find weak algorithm calls or credential-processing behavior.

content.value_statement: Agent frameworks are difficult to compare because implementation choices and reasoning patterns are mixed together. This skill provides a staged forensic protocol for extracting evidence and turning it into design decisions.
actual_capabilities: Separates engineering chassis analysis from cognitive architecture analysis.
Defines a multi-agent workflow for framework, skill, reader, and synthesis roles.
Guides evidence collection for typing, execution, memory, tools, and coordination.
Provides structured output targets for comparison matrices and reference architectures.
Supports resumable analysis through manifest and per-framework state files.
limitations: Requires a codebase mapping workflow or equivalent repository inventory.
Does not include executable orchestration scripts inside this skill package.
Depends on careful agent discipline to keep file reads within assigned scopes.
Produces qualitative architecture guidance, not formal performance benchmarks.

use_cases.title: Compare Agent Frameworks
use_cases.description: Evaluate several agent frameworks against shared architecture dimensions before choosing patterns to reuse.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Agent Runtime
use_cases.description: Convert observed framework decisions into a reference architecture for a new internal agent system.

use_cases.title: Run Technical Due Diligence
use_cases.description: Document how an AI agent project handles state, tools, memory, orchestration, and failure modes.

prompt_templates.title: Start Single-Framework Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin with one repository and a concise report.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use architectural-forensics to analyze this agent framework. Focus on execution flow, state handling, tool interfaces, and the most important design tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Frameworks
prompt_templates.scenario: Identify reusable design choices across two projects.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use architectural-forensics to compare these two agent frameworks. Produce a decision matrix with evidence-backed recommendations for a new system.

prompt_templates.title: Deep Dive on Cognitive Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Study reasoning, memory, and tool behavior in detail.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the cognitive architecture phase of architectural-forensics. Analyze control loops, memory assembly, tool feedback, and multi-agent coordination patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Create Reference Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Synthesize multiple analyses into a design proposal.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use architectural-forensics synthesis to create a reference architecture from completed framework reports. Include key primitives, interfaces, anti-patterns, and implementation priorities.

output_examples.input: Analyze one Python agent framework for implementation design.
output_examples.output: A framework summary that identifies the typing strategy, execution model, extension points, error handling, and recommended patterns to adopt.
A comparison matrix that contrasts async design, memory management, tool schemas, coordination models, and practical tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: Design a new agent framework from prior-art analysis.
output_examples.output: A reference architecture brief with core primitives, interface contracts, execution loop decisions, and anti-patterns to avoid.

best_practices: Run codebase mapping before deeper analysis so every phase uses the same repository inventory.
Keep each reader agent focused on one coherent file cluster with clear boundaries.
Cite evidence from extracted files when making architecture recommendations.
anti_patterns: Do not let coordinator agents read entire source trees when narrower extractors can preserve context.
Do not merge engineering and cognitive findings without labeling the architectural layer.
Do not produce recommendations that lack file-level evidence or clear tradeoffs.

faq.question: What does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes AI agent frameworks across implementation structure, reasoning loops, memory, tools, orchestration, and synthesis outputs.

faq.question: Does it run framework code?
faq.answer: The skill itself is documentation. It describes local mapping commands and analysis workflows that a user or agent may run.

faq.question: Can it compare multiple frameworks?
faq.answer: Yes. The protocol includes framework-level analysis and cross-framework synthesis for matrices, anti-patterns, and reference architectures.

faq.question: Is it only for Python projects?
faq.answer: No. Examples often mention Python, but the analysis categories can apply to other agent framework codebases.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: A mapped repository, known framework paths, and clear goals for comparison or design synthesis produce the best results.

faq.question: What should users review before use?
faq.answer: Users should review any local commands, output paths, and repository scopes before allowing the workflow to read or write files.
